##NAMES=Burroite
##RRUFFID=R250029
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Ca_2_(NH_4_)_2_(V_10_O_28_)·15H_2_O
##LOCALITY=Type sample from Burro Mine, San Miguel County, Colorado, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Orange-yellow, somewhat flattened prisms. Type sample described in Kampf et al (2017)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250029
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=[Ca_1.88_(NH_4_)_1.82_Na_0.18_](H_0.23_V^5+^_10_O_28_)·15H_2_O
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Single crystal, powder profile is calculated
##CELL PARAMETERS=a: 8.779 b: 10.311 c: 12.06 alpha: 96.74 beta: 107.388 gamma: 114.439 volume: 911.2 crystal system: triclinic
##FILETYPE=Raman RAW
##RAMAN WAVELENGTH=532
